title: Semi-Global Block Matching
subtitle: SGBM estimates stereo disparity by matching local image patches while smoothing the result across paths.
summary: The depth estimation project used OpenCV and SGBM to build stereo disparity maps.

externalSources.label: OpenCV: Depth Map from Stereo Images

artifact.title: Disparity changes with correspondence
artifact.description: The demo compresses the core idea: matched points shift more when the object is closer.

sections.heading: Block Matching
body: The algorithm searches for similar patches between the left and right camera images. The horizontal offset between a matching pair becomes disparity.

sections.heading: Semi-Global Smoothing
body: Pure local matching is noisy. SGBM adds penalties so neighboring disparities prefer to vary smoothly unless image evidence says otherwise.

pitfalls: Expecting good matches on blank walls.

quiz.question: What does stereo block matching try to find?
options: Similar patches across left and right images
quiz.explanation: Disparity comes from matching corresponding visual patches.
